supersedes: Prior self-eval at commit 16b66d6 (generator self-evaluation). This re-evaluation was performed in a fresh evaluator context per harness structural-separation principle.
summary: All 21 contract criteria PASS. Sprint 1's contract was bootstrapped onto an already-built Sprint 2 codebase: Sprint 2 had delivered the full scaffold (tile/floor/entity/game/render modules + 8-dir movement + headless/seed CLI). Sprint 1's diff against the sprint-02 branch is small (a Game.from_seed factory, an updated package docstring, and a new tests/test_sprint01.py with 23 contract-mapped tests). The harness ran the full pytest suite (44 passed, 0 failed) and verified the headless CLI manually.

evidence.diffStat: tests/test_sprint01.py +337, whisperdeep/__init__.py +13/-2, whisperdeep/game.py +18 (Game.from_seed factory). Sprint 1's actual code addition is just the from_seed factory; the rest of the implementation was completed during sprint-02.
evidence.headless: `python -m whisperdeep --headless --seed 1` -> exit 0, stdout = 40 lines, stderr = 0 bytes, no traceback.
evidence.frameContents: Unique non-newline glyphs in headless frame: {'#', '+', '.', '>', '@'} -- subset of legend {'#', '.', '+', '<', '>', '@'}. '@' count == 1, '#' count = 2174 (border walls).
evidence.reproducibility: Two runs of `python -m whisperdeep --headless --seed 1` produced byte-identical stdout (verified with diff -q).
evidence.directApi: tiles.wall().glyph='#'/walkable=False; tiles.floor().glyph='.'/walkable=True. Floor(20,10).width==20, .height==10, .in_bounds(-1,0)==False, .in_bounds(0,0)==True. Player(5,7).x==5, .y==7, .glyph='@', isinstance Entity=True. Game.from_seed(1).player at (22,35), in_bounds. game.turns starts at 0; after 2 OOB try_move calls (both return False, position unchanged at (0,0)), turns advanced by 2 (>=1 per action -- C14 satisfied). render_floor(Floor(10,5)) returns 5 lines, each exactly 10 chars.

criteriaResults.evidence: `whisperdeep/__init__.py` exists; `python -c 'import whisperdeep'` exits 0; __version__='0.2.0' exposed.
`whisperdeep/__main__.py` exists; `python -m whisperdeep --help` prints argparse usage and exits 0.
All five module files present and importable: whisperdeep/tiles.py, floor.py, entity.py, game.py, render.py. Verified via `from whisperdeep import tiles, floor, entity, game, render` succeeding.
tiles.wall() -> glyph='#', walkable=False. tiles.floor() -> glyph='.', walkable=True. TileKind enum exposes WALL/FLOOR/DOOR/UPSTAIRS/DOWNSTAIRS.
Floor(20,10).width==20, .height==10. in_bounds(-1,0)=False, in_bounds(0,0)=True. Out-of-bounds is detectable via in_bounds(); .get(-1,0) raises IndexError -- defensive, not silently wrong.
Player is a subclass of Entity (verified via isinstance). Player(5,7).x==5, .y==7, .glyph='@'.
Game.from_seed(1) constructs without error. Returned Game has .floor and .player; player at (22,35) is inside floor.in_bounds. Factory was the explicit Sprint 1 addition (whisperdeep/game.py:36-52).
render_floor(Floor(10,5)) -> 5 lines, each exactly 10 characters wide. Verified live.
Headless frame from Game.from_seed(1): frame.count('@') == 1, and the '@' is at the player's (px,py) -- pytest test_c9_frame_renderer_places_player_glyph_exactly_once passes.
Unique non-newline frame chars = {'#','+','.','>','@'} -- subset of legend {'#','.','+','<','>','@'}. '#' present (2174 occurrences), '@' present (1).
test_c11_eight_directional_movement_on_open_floor PASSED: from center of an 11x11 open arena, all 8 (dx,dy) deltas in {-1,0,1}^2 \ {(0,0)} succeed and update position by exactly the requested offset.
test_c12_wall_bump_is_noop PASSED: try_move(1,0) into a wall returns False and leaves position unchanged. Also covered by test_player_cannot_walk_through_walls.
Verified live: Game with player at (0,0), try_move(-1,0) -> False, try_move(0,-1) -> False, no exception raised, position remains (0,0).
Verified live: game.turns starts at 0; after a wall-bump and an OOB attempt, turns delta = 2 (>=1 per action). test_c14_turn_counter_advances_on_actions PASSED.
`python -m whisperdeep --headless --seed 1` -> exit 0; stdout 40 lines, contains '@' (count=1) and '#' (>1).
Two `python -m whisperdeep --headless --seed 1` invocations produced byte-identical stdout (diff -q reports identical). --seed argument is parsed and threaded into World construction.
`python -m pytest -q` collects and passes 44 tests across tests/test_generator.py, test_game.py, test_sprint01.py. Threshold was >=8.

tile walkability: test_c4_wall_tile_glyph_and_walkable + test_c4_floor_tile_glyph_and_walkable + test_c18_tile_walkability_wall_vs_floor. Floor dims: test_c5_floor_dimensions_and_in_bounds. Player movement: test_c11_eight_directional_movement_on_open_floor + test_game_launches_and_accepts_a_move. Wall bump no-op: test_c12_wall_bump_is_noop + test_player_cannot_walk_through_walls.

docs/whisperdeep.md contains: Quick start with `python -m whisperdeep --seed 1` and `--headless` invocation; Glyph legend table listing #/./+/<>/@; Command-line flags table documenting --seed and --headless. All three required topics present.

No pyproject.toml/requirements.txt declaring third-party deps. test_c20_no_third_party_runtime_imports PASSED -- heuristic guard against numpy/tcod/rich/click/requests etc. finds none. Only stdlib imports (argparse, dataclasses, enum, random, sys, typing).
`python -m whisperdeep --headless --seed 1` returncode=0, stderr 0 bytes, no 'Traceback' on stderr.

holisticScores.notes: Project type 'general' -- general-purpose holistic dimensions used. Correctness: all 21 criteria verified by both pytest suite and live manual checks. Completeness: every contract criterion has explicit test coverage; deliverables list fully met. Code quality: clean module separation matches the contract's stated separation-of-concerns goal; Game.from_seed is a reasonable, well-documented factory. Test quality: 44 passing tests, contract-mapped (each Cn has at least one test_cn_* test). Documentation: docs/whisperdeep.md covers run command + legend + flags. Stability: zero stderr, zero traceback, byte-identical seeded runs.

recommendations: Sprint 1's contract was effectively retro-validated against the Sprint 2 codebase. This is acceptable because Sprint 2 implemented (and exceeded) Sprint 1's deliverables, and an explicit contract-mapped test file (tests/test_sprint01.py) was added to verify each C-ID. Future sprints should be implemented against their own contracts in the intended order.
The interactive run loop (cli.run_interactive) uses input() rather than a real TUI; this is in line with the contract's explicit allowance for a stdout-frame renderer and out-of-scope list.
